talk_metadata.talk_title: Immunological considerations of radioligand therapy and B7H3‑targeted antibody BetaBART
rhetorical_signals: Speaker said: "the circulating two is a potential pseudo target, in effect, a decoy or a sink that's not widely appreciated that could really confound therapy"
Speaker said: "we see significant long‑term survivals with just a single injection of 100 µCi of the yttrium‑90 beta emitting MIL‑33B"
Speaker said: "cold antibody by itself as monotherapy has no effect. All the animals die"

vulnerability_identified.mechanistic_blindspot: Circulating soluble 2‑Ig isoform acts as a decoy sink, reducing delivery of radioligand to tumor‑bound 4‑Ig isoform
vulnerability_identified.unexplained_resistance_quote: Speaker said: "we don't have direct evidence that the circulating 2‑Ig isoform limits tumor uptake in patients"

trial_dilution_risk.flawed_enrollment_criteria: Enrolling all‑comers with B7H3 IHC positivity without stratifying for 4‑Ig vs 2‑Ig isoform expression or circulating soluble B7H3 levels
trial_dilution_risk.missing_biomarker: Quantitative serum 2‑Ig B7H3 level or tumor 4‑Ig specific expression
trial_dilution_risk.statistical_concern: Single‑arm design limits ability to discern contribution of isoform sink versus true drug activity

competitive_moat_weakness.vulnerable_ip_or_chemistry: Full‑length IgG antibody scaffold targeting B7H3 4‑Ig
competitive_moat_weakness.ip_vulnerability_note: Speaker disclosed that the antibody has 300‑fold selectivity for 4‑Ig over 2‑Ig, but the human‑specific 4‑Ig isoform is absent in standard mouse models, limiting preclinical translatability
competitive_moat_weakness.clinical_strategy_weakness: Pursuing a broad pan‑tumor label without biomarker‑driven patient selection may dilute signal due to isoform variability

cognitive_dissonance: Speaker showed that the MIL‑33B antibody alone (cold) has no anti‑tumor effect, yet concluded that the Fc domain may still engage FCR receptors and contribute to immune priming as a single‑agent therapy

key_data_gaps: No biomarker‑stratified analysis of 4‑Ig vs 2‑Ig expression
Lack of comparative arm with standard of care

crispro_opportunity.description: Develop a CrisPRO‑enabled assay to quantify circulating soluble B7H3 2‑Ig isoform and tumor‑specific 4‑Ig expression to select patients most likely to benefit from BetaBART
crispro_opportunity.transcript_evidence: Speaker said: "the circulating two is a potential pseudo target, in effect, a decoy or a sink that's not widely appreciated"
crispro_opportunity.crispro_angle: CrisPRO can integrate proteomic and transcriptomic data to model isoform‑specific binding and predict patient‑level sink effects

crispro_opportunity.description: Redesign the BetaBART trial to include a biomarker‑enriched cohort (high 4‑Ig, low soluble 2‑Ig) and a control arm receiving the cold antibody plus external beam radiation to isolate Fc‑mediated effects
crispro_opportunity.transcript_evidence: Speaker noted that "cold antibody by itself as monotherapy has no effect" yet the Fc may still play a role
crispro_opportunity.crispro_angle: CrisPRO can simulate trial outcomes under different stratification scenarios to prioritize designs

crispro_opportunity.description: Use CrisPRO to design next‑generation anti‑B7H3 constructs that avoid the soluble 2‑Ig decoy (e.g., bispecific formats or engineered Fc‑silencing) to overcome the identified resistance mechanism
crispro_opportunity.transcript_evidence: Speaker highlighted the decoy sink as a confounder for therapy efficacy
crispro_opportunity.crispro_angle: Computational protein design within CrisPRO can generate variants with enhanced 4‑Ig specificity and reduced off‑target binding

cited_competitors.name: Zach Morris (University of Wisconsin)
cited_competitors.context: Compared small‑molecule NM600 radioligand to the MIL‑33B antibody in preclinical models

cited_competitors.name: ADC programs targeting B7H3
cited_competitors.context: Mentioned as parallel development to radioligand approach

unresolved_questions: How does the level of circulating soluble 2‑Ig B7H3 correlate with clinical response in patients receiving BetaBART?
Can Fc engineering improve anti‑tumor immunity without increasing toxicity?

talk_metadata.talk_title: Granzyme‑B PET imaging platform for monitoring immune activation during PSMA‑targeted radioligand therapy
rhetorical_signals: Speaker said: "we see a very compelling correlation between PET avid lesions and other orthogonal clinical features of response"
Speaker said: "we don't know when to run the granzyme scan"
Speaker said: "we occasionally see uptake in patients that are not receiving IO"

vulnerability_identified.mechanistic_blindspot: Potential off‑target activation by other proteases (e.g., caspases) in necrotic or inflamed tissue leading to false‑positive signals

trial_dilution_risk.trial_name_or_nct: Granzyme‑B PET sub‑study within PLUVICTO + pembrolizumab trial
trial_dilution_risk.target_biology: Imaging of granzyme‑B release as a surrogate for CD8 T‑cell activation
trial_dilution_risk.flawed_enrollment_criteria: No predefined imaging time‑points based on pharmacodynamics; scans performed opportunistically leading to heterogeneous data
trial_dilution_risk.missing_biomarker: Concurrent measurement of serum granzyme‑B activity to validate PET signal
trial_dilution_risk.static_vs_dynamic_failure: No serial biopsies or ctDNA to track evolving resistance
trial_dilution_risk.statistical_concern: Small patient numbers (case series) limit statistical power

competitive_moat_weakness.ip_vulnerability_note: Speaker disclosed the peptide originates from a frog antimicrobial sequence; core chemistry (DOTA‑Cu‑64) is widely used, suggesting limited proprietary protection
competitive_moat_weakness.scale_or_manufacturing_bottleneck: Peptide synthesis and radiolabeling require GMP facilities; scalability not demonstrated
competitive_moat_weakness.clinical_strategy_weakness: Reliance on a narrow clinical niche (PSMA‑RLT + IO) may limit broader adoption

cognitive_dissonance: Speaker showed that granzyme‑B PET signal increases in responding lesions, yet also admitted "we don't know when to run the granzyme scan" indicating a gap between mechanistic confidence and practical clinical implementation

key_data_gaps: Lack of head‑to‑head comparison with PSMA PET response
No systematic correlation with peripheral blood granzyme‑B assays
Limited data on specificity in non‑IO patients

crispro_opportunity.description: Implement CrisPRO to define optimal imaging windows based on kinetic modeling of granzyme‑B release after PSMA‑RLT
crispro_opportunity.transcript_evidence: Speaker said: "we don't know when to run the granzyme scan"
crispro_opportunity.crispro_angle: CrisPRO can integrate PK/PD data to predict peak granzyme activity and schedule PET scans accordingly

crispro_opportunity.description: Leverage CrisPRO’s protease‑specificity prediction to engineer next‑generation GRIP probes with reduced cross‑reactivity to caspases
crispro_opportunity.transcript_evidence: Speaker said: "we focus on other things that we think have potentially granzyme‑like biochemical activity... there’s remarkably little activity"
crispro_opportunity.crispro_angle: Computational protease substrate design within CrisPRO can improve selectivity

crispro_opportunity.description: Design a randomized arm comparing granzyme‑B PET‑guided therapy adjustments versus standard of care in PSMA‑RLT + IO patients
crispro_opportunity.transcript_evidence: Speaker noted the current study is a small case series without control arm
crispro_opportunity.crispro_angle: CrisPRO can simulate trial outcomes under different imaging‑guided decision rules

cited_competitors.name: Standard PSMA PET imaging
cited_competitors.context: Used as sanity check to confirm lesions are cancerous

unresolved_questions: What is the quantitative relationship between granzyme‑B PET SUV and actual intratumoral CD8 activity?
Can granzyme‑B PET predict response earlier than PSA decline or PSMA PET changes?

talk_metadata.talk_title: Immunostimulatory radioligand therapy to enhance checkpoint blockade and CAR‑T efficacy
rhetorical_signals: Speaker said: "when we gave external beam to the whole mouse, we lost that effect"
Speaker said: "systemic lymphodepletion blunts the benefit of checkpoint blockade"
Speaker said: "low dose TRT can act as a conditioning agent for adoptive T‑cell therapy"

vulnerability_identified.failing_compound_or_target: Systemic high‑dose TRT (e.g., full‑length antibody‑based radioligand)
vulnerability_identified.mechanistic_blindspot: Radiation delivered to lymphoid organs depletes CD4/CD8 cells, negating immune checkpoint synergy
vulnerability_identified.ghost_responder_admission: Speaker noted that 50 % of mice responded while 50 % progressed, implying a subset benefit
vulnerability_identified.hidden_tox_signal: Speaker said: "when we gave two and a half gray to the whole mouse, we lost that effect"

trial_dilution_risk.trial_name_or_nct: Preclinical TRT + checkpoint combination studies (CT26, B78, 4T1 models)
trial_dilution_risk.target_biology: Low‑dose yttrium‑90 or actinium‑225 NM600 radioligand plus anti‑PD‑1/CTLA‑4
trial_dilution_risk.flawed_enrollment_criteria: No stratification for tumor immunogenicity; mixed hot and cold models combined in same analysis
trial_dilution_risk.missing_biomarker: Baseline tumor infiltrating lymphocyte (TIL) density or PD‑L1 status
trial_dilution_risk.static_vs_dynamic_failure: No serial ctDNA or imaging to monitor emergence of resistance
trial_dilution_risk.statistical_concern: Small animal cohort sizes (n≈5‑10) limit reproducibility

competitive_moat_weakness.ip_vulnerability_note: Speaker described NM600 as a small‑molecule lipid‑raft binder; similar analogs are publicly disclosed, suggesting limited IP moat
competitive_moat_weakness.scale_or_manufacturing_bottleneck: Synthesis of high‑purity NM600 at GMP scale not demonstrated
competitive_moat_weakness.clinical_strategy_weakness: Reliance on low‑dose regimens may limit monotherapy efficacy and require combination partners for meaningful clinical benefit

cognitive_dissonance: Speaker showed that the full‑length B7H3 antibody MIL‑33B alone can generate immune memory, yet also concluded that "cold antibody by itself has no effect" in control experiments, creating a contradiction between observed adaptive immunity and lack of monotherapy activity

key_data_gaps: No human clinical trial data presented
Lack of pharmacokinetic/dosimetry data for systemic lymphocyte exposure

crispro_opportunity.description: Use CrisPRO to model optimal dose‑fractionation schedules that maximize tumor dose while sparing lymphoid organs for checkpoint blockade
crispro_opportunity.transcript_evidence: Speaker said: "when we gave two and a half gray to the whole mouse, we lost that effect"
crispro_opportunity.crispro_angle: CrisPRO can simulate organ‑level radiation exposure and predict immune cell preservation

crispro_opportunity.description: Incorporate baseline TIL or PD‑L1 biomarker stratification into future TRT + IO trials to reduce heterogeneity
crispro_opportunity.transcript_evidence: Speaker noted mixed responder/non‑responder outcomes in the same model
crispro_opportunity.crispro_angle: CrisPRO can evaluate enrichment strategies and estimate power gains

crispro_opportunity.description: Re‑engineer NM600 with a cleavable linker that reduces off‑target lymphoid uptake, addressing the identified toxicity limitation
crispro_opportunity.transcript_evidence: Speaker highlighted systemic lymphodepletion as a problem for checkpoint synergy
crispro_opportunity.crispro_angle: Computational chemistry within CrisPRO can design linkers that preferentially release radioisotope in tumor microenvironment

cited_competitors.name: FAP‑targeted radioligands
cited_competitors.context: Benchmarked against RIPS platform for tumor AUC

cited_competitors.name: GD2‑targeted antibody radioligand
cited_competitors.context: Compared efficacy; noted higher systemic lymphodepletion

unresolved_questions: What is the minimal tumor dose of NM600 required to achieve immune priming without systemic lymphodepletion?
Can the timing of TRT relative to checkpoint inhibitor dosing be optimized to enhance memory T‑cell formation?
